news_summary: Efforts are underway to preserve the Hubble Space Telescope, ensuring continued exploration and discovery in space.
news_summary_fr: Des efforts sont actuellement déployés pour préserver le télescope spatial Hubble, afin de garantir la poursuite de l'exploration et des découvertes spatiales.
news_summary_es: Se están llevando a cabo iniciativas para preservar el telescopio espacial Hubble, con el fin de garantizar que continúen la exploración y los descubrimientos espaciales.

articles.title: Save Hubble: the race to preserve the space telescope kicks off
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 07 July 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-02000-xThe orbiting observatory’s unique vision and public support continue to be crucial for understanding the Universe.
articles.content:
Remnants of an exploded star, captured by the Hubble Space Telesope.Credit: NASA, ESA, and F. Summers, G. Bacon, Z. Levay, and L. Frattare (Viz 3D Team, STScI)

The Hubble Space Telescope is an audacious expression of human curiosity. Since its launch in 1990, it has done much more than just expand humanity’s scientific reach: it has changed how people see themselves in the cosmos.

Space diplomacy: bridging the operating gaps between myriad missions

Now, Hubble’s future hangs in the balance. A NASA working group is considering whether to extend the observatory’s mission into the 2030s by boosting it into a higher, more stable orbit, or to send a robotic mission to decommission it, plunging the telescope into the ocean.

Each option’s risks, benefits and costs need to be considered. Calls for white papers and pitches to define Hubble’s science priorities for the next decade will conclude in July. Later this year, the working group will share its advice with NASA and the US Congress, which will inform further plans.

I submitted a white paper arguing that Hubble should remain operational for as long as technically feasible. It was an easy case to make, but the future of this iconic telescope is not guaranteed. Scientists and the public must back this remarkable project into its fifth decade.

What’s at stake? Hubble’s unique ability to complement all of NASA’s current and planned scientific missions until 2040. The ultraviolet and optical wavelengths that the telescope captures are essential to understanding how and when galaxies, stars and planets formed.

Historic Artemis II Moon fly-by: Nature’s live coverage as it happened

Hubble has swept us beyond human imagination. It tore back the curtain on an exciting incandescent Universe — not just for a handful of astronomers, but for everyone. Its images are visceral, both beautiful and terrifying at the same time, and it revealed the raw interplay of matter and energy on scales that words could never fully express.

Its chain of discoveries reinvigorated modern astrophysics. The deepest optical image of the Universe ever taken revealed how galaxies have evolved throughout cosmic history. Measurements of supernovae showed that the Universe’s expansion is accelerating under the influence of the mysterious force known as dark energy. Hubble mapped the cycle of stellar birth and death with unprecedented clarity and was the first telescope to measure the atmosphere of a planet orbiting a star other than the Sun.

The mission prioritizes transparency, collaboration and public engagement. Its data have been used by astronomers around the world. Far from obsolete, its instruments are still cutting edge, operational and in demand — proposals outnumber availability by a factor of six-to-one. And it fills a crucial niche in NASA’s suite of flagship observatories.

Hubble’s blue–UV view of the Universe complements that of the James Webb Space Telescope, which captures infrared light with comparable resolution and field of view. Broad coverage of the electromagnetic spectrum is essential for characterizing extrasolar planets, galaxies and black holes. A successor to Hubble, the Habitable Worlds Observatory, won’t be launched before 2040.
articles.reasoning: The article highlights the ongoing efforts to preserve the Hubble Space Telescope, a critical scientific instrument with broad impact on humanity's understanding of the Universe. It discusses the tangible benefits of extending Hubble's mission, its unique scientific contributions, and the public and scientific community's support, all of which represent significant positive real-world impact in space exploration and science.
articles.personality_title: NASA considers ways to extend Hubble Space Telescope’s mission into the 2030s
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – The Hubble Space Telescope has been orbiting Earth since 1990, capturing stunning images and important data about the Universe. It has helped scientists understand how galaxies, stars, and planets form. Now, NASA is deciding what to do next as Hubble’s future is uncertain.

**What happened** – NASA formed a group to explore options for Hubble’s future. They are thinking about either moving Hubble to a higher orbit to keep it working longer or safely ending its mission by sending a robot to bring it down into the ocean. Scientists and the public have shared ideas on how Hubble should be used in the coming years. The group will give their recommendations to NASA and the US Congress later this year.

**Impact** – Hubble is special because it sees ultraviolet and visible light, which other space telescopes like the James Webb don’t capture as well. This makes Hubble vital for studying many space objects and events. Its discoveries have changed our view of the Universe and inspired many people. Even after more than 30 years, Hubble’s instruments are still in great shape and many scientists want to use it.

**What’s next step** – NASA will review the advice from the working group and decide whether to extend Hubble’s mission or retire it. If extended, Hubble could keep helping scientists learn about the Universe until the 2030s, filling an important gap before the next big telescope launches around 2040.

**One-sentence takeaway** – NASA is carefully planning how to keep the Hubble Space Telescope working longer to continue its unique role in exploring the Universe and supporting scientific discoveries.
articles.personality_title_fr: La NASA envisage de prolonger la mission du télescope spatial Hubble jusqu’aux années 2030
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Le télescope spatial Hubble orbite autour de la Terre depuis 1990, capturant des images impressionnantes et des données importantes sur l’Univers. Il a aidé les scientifiques à comprendre comment se forment les galaxies, les étoiles et les planètes. Aujourd’hui, la NASA doit décider de l’avenir d’Hubble.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – La NASA a créé un groupe pour étudier les options pour l’avenir d’Hubble. Ils envisagent soit de déplacer Hubble en orbite plus haute pour prolonger sa durée de vie, soit de mettre fin à sa mission en envoyant un robot pour le faire tomber dans l’océan. Des scientifiques et le public ont partagé leurs idées pour l’utilisation future d’Hubble. Le groupe donnera ses recommandations à la NASA et au Congrès américain plus tard cette année.

**Impact** – Hubble est spécial car il observe la lumière ultraviolette et visible, ce que d’autres télescopes comme le James Webb ne font pas aussi bien. Cela rend Hubble essentiel pour étudier de nombreux objets et événements spatiaux. Ses découvertes ont changé notre vision de l’Univers et inspiré beaucoup de personnes. Même après plus de 30 ans, les instruments d’Hubble fonctionnent toujours bien et beaucoup de scientifiques souhaitent l’utiliser.

**Prochaine étape** – La NASA examinera les conseils du groupe de travail et décidera de prolonger la mission d’Hubble ou de la terminer. Si elle est prolongée, Hubble pourrait continuer à aider les scientifiques à explorer l’Univers jusqu’aux années 2030, comblant un vide important avant le lancement du prochain grand télescope vers 2040.

**Résumé en une phrase** – La NASA planifie soigneusement comment maintenir le télescope spatial Hubble en fonctionnement plus longtemps pour continuer son rôle unique dans l’exploration de l’Univers et soutenir les découvertes scientifiques.
articles.personality_title_es: La NASA considera extender la misión del telescopio espacial Hubble hasta la década de 2030
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – El telescopio espacial Hubble ha estado orbitando la Tierra desde 1990, capturando imágenes impresionantes y datos importantes sobre el Universo. Ha ayudado a los científicos a entender cómo se forman las galaxias, estrellas y planetas. Ahora, la NASA debe decidir qué hacer con el futuro de Hubble.

**Qué pasó** – La NASA formó un grupo para estudiar las opciones para el futuro de Hubble. Están pensando en mover a Hubble a una órbita más alta para que funcione más tiempo, o en terminar su misión enviando un robot para llevarlo al océano. Científicos y el público han compartido ideas sobre cómo usar Hubble en los próximos años. El grupo dará sus recomendaciones a la NASA y al Congreso de Estados Unidos este año.

**Impacto** – Hubble es especial porque puede ver la luz ultravioleta y visible, algo que otros telescopios como el James Webb no hacen tan bien. Esto hace que Hubble sea muy importante para estudiar muchos objetos y eventos en el espacio. Sus descubrimientos han cambiado nuestra forma de ver el Universo e inspirado a muchas personas. Después de más de 30 años, los instrumentos de Hubble siguen funcionando bien y muchos científicos quieren usarlo.

**Próximo paso** – La NASA revisará las recomendaciones del grupo y decidirá si extiende la misión de Hubble o la termina. Si se extiende, Hubble podría seguir ayudando a los científicos a explorar el Universo hasta la década de 2030, cubriendo un espacio importante antes de que se lance el próximo gran telescopio alrededor de 2040.

**Conclusión en una frase** – La NASA está planificando cuidadosamente cómo mantener el telescopio espacial Hubble funcionando más tiempo para continuar su papel único en la exploración del Universo y apoyar descubrimientos científicos.
articles.image_prompt: An intricately detailed painting of the Hubble Space Telescope orbiting serenely above Earth, bathed in soft, warm light, with a vibrant cosmic backdrop featuring swirling remnants of an exploded star and distant galaxies glowing gently in ultraviolet and optical hues, symbolizing humanity’s enduring curiosity and the telescope’s vital role in unveiling the mysteries of the universe.
